title: Linear Equations
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Linear Equations.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Formulating word problems into systems of linear variables, Integer constraints constraints (Diophantine equations).

content_sections.title: Linear Equations
content_sections.content:
### Arithmetic, Totients & Combinatorics
Quantitative Aptitude focuses on number relationships, algebraic systems, and probability calculations.

#### Euler's Totient Theorem
For coprimes $a$ and $n$, the remainder of $a^{\phi(n)} \pmod n$ is $1$, where $\phi(n)$ is Euler's totient (count of numbers coprime to $n$ up to $n$):
$$\phi(n) = n \prod_{p \mid n} \left(1 - \frac{1}{p}\right)$$
For primes, $\phi(p) = p - 1$, yielding Fermat's Little Theorem: $a^{p-1} \equiv 1 \pmod p$.

#### Product Consistency Constraint
If $A \times B = C$ and $C$ remains constant, a fractional increase in $A$ by $x/y$ requires a fractional decrease in $B$ by $\frac{x}{x + y}$ to maintain product consistency.

#### Stars-and-Bars Partitions
To distribute $n$ identical objects among $k$ distinct bins:
*   *Empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n + k - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.
*   *No empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.

### Depth Analysis: Linear Equations
In production configurations, we analyze how Formulating word problems into systems of linear variables, Integer constraints constraints (Diophantine equations) affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Linear Equations
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Linear Equations is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Linear Equations
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Linear Equations loops.
